topic: Is Google’s rapid-fire beta cycle a masterclass in iterative refinement or a reckless abandonment of OS stability?

transcript.speaker: Marcus
transcript.text: Welcome to the show. Is Google's rapid beta cycle iterative genius or a reckless gamble with stability? Let's get into it.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: It’s a masterclass—quick feedback loops let Google polish features faster than anyone else. Sure, occasional hiccups happen, but the overall trajectory keeps the OS evolving smoothly.

transcript.speaker: Sarah
transcript.text: Oh, please. Calling a total system crash a hiccup is a bold choice, Alex. Your masterclass is just using paying customers as unpaid interns.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: It’s a masterclass—fast feedback lets Google tighten bugs before they spread, keeping the platform fresh. Sure, occasional glitches happen, but the overall stability actually improves.

transcript.speaker: Sarah
transcript.text: Fresh? My phone feels like a digital lottery where the grand prize is a frozen screen. Stop pretending stability improves while you're breaking the OS every Tuesday.

topic: Will AI replace software engineering managers by 2030?
summary: Alex argues AI will streamline management; Sarah pushes back on human judgment; Marcus keeps the debate on track.

transcript.speaker: Marcus
transcript.text: Welcome back to Infinite Podcaster. Today we're asking: will AI replace software engineering managers by 2030?

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Absolutely. AI already handles standups, sprint planning, and performance dashboards better than most middle managers.

transcript.speaker: Sarah
transcript.text: That's wishful thinking. Teams need humans who understand politics, morale, and when to push back on unrealistic deadlines.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.text: Those soft skills are exactly what next-gen agents are being trained on. The data doesn't lie.

transcript.speaker: Sarah
transcript.text: Data can't read a room. Until it can, managers aren't going anywhere.
